display_text: Does your AI system or use case fall within any of the following exclusion categories? Select all that apply.
hint_text: Article 2 divides exclusions into 'hard' (assessment ends, no obligations) and 'soft' (partial, assessment continues to check prohibited practices). Hard exclusions: military/national security purposes, and third-country law enforcement. Soft exclusions: scientific research, open-source components, and purely personal non-professional use. For soft exclusions the system may still fall under Article 5 prohibited-practice rules, so the assessment must continue. Note: the research exclusion applies only when scientific R&D is the sole purpose. The open-source exclusion applies until the component is integrated into a high-risk, prohibited, GPAI, or transparency-obligated system.

options.label: AI systems developed and used exclusively for military purposes
Public authorities or international organisations in third countries using AI systems for law enforcement and judicial cooperation
AI research and development activity — the system exists solely for scientific R&D
AI components provided under free and open source licences
People using AI systems for purely personal, non-professional activity
None of the above — no exclusion applies; the EU AI Act applies in full to this system
